summary:
DIVINE CALLING FOR HEALTH: Brandon explains that he received a directive from God to prioritize his physical health and eliminate excess weight. Acknowledging a lifelong struggle with poor eating habits, stress eating, and an addiction to unhealthy foods like chocolate, chips, and candy, Brandon views this issue as a spiritual stronghold. He currently weighs 304 pounds at a height of 5 feet 11 inches and has set a definitive goal to lose 100 pounds during the year 2024 to reach 204 pounds.

DIETARY STRATEGY AND EXERCISE PLAN: Brandon outlines his approach to achieving his weight loss goal by adopting a ketogenic and carnivore-based diet. He plans to eliminate all sweets, breads, pastas, and sugary sodas from his daily intake, focusing instead on meat consumption. Alongside dietary modifications, Brandon emphasizes the importance of incorporating regular exercise into his routine to improve his heart health and overall physical condition, with the intention of permanently keeping the weight off rather than engaging in a cycle of yo-yo dieting.

PREVIOUS WEIGHT LOSS EXPERIENCES: Brandon recounts a previous period in his life where he successfully lost 80 pounds over the course of a year. During that time, he utilized a program called Sanford by Profile, eating five small meals a day consisting of 1,000 calories alongside lean and green options. However, after deciding to discontinue the expensive program, Brandon regained the lost weight over the following year by reverting to consuming fast food, specifically mentioning his fondness for fries from Chick-fil-A and McDonald's.

PERSONAL MOTIVATION AND SPIRITUAL DISCIPLINE: Brandon notes that turning 44 years old has made him acutely aware of his family's history of hereditary health issues, prompting him to take his physical longevity seriously. He relies on scriptures related to self-control, framing his dietary discipline as a fruit of the Spirit necessary to fulfill his life's purpose. Brandon discusses praying for supernatural assistance in breaking food addictions, shifting his mindset to view food strictly as fuel for sustaining his ministry rather than a source of comfort, and declaring spiritual authority over unhealthy physical cravings.
rawData: [January 02, 2024]-[Lose weight with me in 2024]-[https://youtu.be/53k9Cj8j4ao]-[Hey, good morning, family. I'm excited to be with you all again today. I'm thinking of bringing on a challenge—let's do something fun together. It doesn't always have to be serious when I come on here preaching; this is just something that is on my heart. I feel like it's easier when we're a team with a sense of camaraderie. The Lord really put it on my heart one day while I was praying, and He said to me, 'Brandon, I really want you to take care of yourself and get rid of some weight.' I want to get rid of it; I don't say 'lose' it because I don't want to find it again. I want to get rid of it and not go back into this bad cycle of eating poorly and not taking care of my body this way. It's something that I have struggled with my whole life: stress eating and always nibbling on chocolate, chips, or candy. I asked the Lord, 'I have an addiction, Lord, toward bad food,' and it's been a stronghold. To be real with y'all, we all have stuff we're going through, but this is something that I deal with and it's been a real issue with me. To be real with you, I'm 304 pounds right now. I'm 5'11" and 304 pounds, and that's too big. The Lord said, 'Brandon, I need you to lose 100 pounds.' When I get down to 204 pounds, we'll see where we are then, but I'm believing God that in 2024 I'm going to do the things I need to do to achieve that goal. I know that it's not going to just fall off of me by eating the way I still did, so I'm going to change it up. People have given me emails about keto and the carnivore diet, so there are a lot of people that feel like I need to lose weight; it's not just the Lord. I'm going to start off doing ketogenic and I believe I can do this doing keto and more carnivore—not doing strictly keto, but eating the meats and cutting sweets out completely. I'm not going to do sweets, breads, pastas, or things like that—no cookies, I'm cutting it all out. I'm cutting out the treats and no sugary sodas. I'm going to really fine-tune this thing: exercise, take care of my heart, take care of my body, and focus on being a healthier me in 2024. Every week, I'll come on here and tell you how much I've lost or even if I've gained something, but I'm believing God that the scale is going the other way. My wife said, 'Brandon, you're handsome regardless of how you look, but it would be good if you lost 100 pounds just for your health.' So, I'm going to do this, but not only lose it, I'm going to keep it off so I don't want to find it ever again. I don't want to be on the roller coaster diet. You all saw me in that one video, my tribulation video, when I went to the throne room of the Lord. I was doing packet foods called Sanford by Profile. I was eating five times a day—1,000 calories a day plus a lean and green—and I lost 80 pounds over that year because it was just on my heart. I was heavy and I needed to do it. I got a weight loss coach and he was really good, a nice guy, and every single week I weighed in and they kept on me. Well, I didn't want to pay the $1,500 again to do the program, so I went away from it and I gained all that weight back over the course of a year just eating trash again. Chick-fil-A is fine, but I was eating the fries and McDonald's fries. I don't want nobody trying to come after me like I'm talking bad about your company; I'm just telling y'all I like your food, but I know you have healthier alternatives, Chick-fil-A. It doesn't taste as good, though, Brandon. I'm just trying to say your fries are good and, golly, you get me all tickled. I'm not trying to pick at your companies; I'm just saying that's not the direction I need to go anymore. We love you, Chick-fil-A; you're a blessing. Put your weight goals down in the comments. Let's do it together. You don't even have to say if you're female or male because girls are real sensitive to that stuff, but you can write down, 'I'm believing God for 20 pounds this year.' Every single week we'll weigh in. I'll try to make a video, but I'm trying to reprogram my mind to think healthy. It's not an excuse anymore. I'm 43 years old, turning 44 this year, and it's really serious. You have to start taking care of yourself, especially regarding hereditary issues your family has dealt with for years. You've just got to say, 'No, that's not me. I'm not going home early.' I'm going to stick around; God has a plan for me. I have things I need to do, and so I'm not going to let weight stand in my way anymore. No more strongholds. I'm finding scriptures about self-control: God has not given me a spirit of fear, but of power, love, and of a sound mind. He gives you a spirit of self-control. One of the fruits of the Spirit is self-control. I don't need those things. This is just something I'm talking to y'all about, just being real. I hope this blessed y'all. I know it's kind of quirky, but I'm praying and believing God that I look back at this video this time next year and see the turnaround. Father, I thank You for every single person here. If they need help with their weight just like me, Lord, I petition You. I thank You that this is the year of change, the year that we're not going back into that stupid pattern of eating wrong and abusing food. Father, I thank You, Lord, that we use food as fuel to maintain us; it's not for comfort or enjoyment. I'm looking at it, Father, as a place of sustaining my ministry and doing what God's called me to do. It's a different mindset. Father, I thank You, Lord, that every single one of us will overcome; we overcome by the blood of the Lamb and by the word of our testimony. I thank You, Father, that there will be a great turnaround of events in each one of our lives. When we look back next year, we're going to say, 'look at what the Lord has done,' and we give You all the glory, honor, and praise for it in the name of Jesus. We ask You, God, that You help us and give us supernatural weight loss. What may take some people years to do, God, may You accelerate it in our bodies to help us be healthy for You. I take authority over the cravings for sugar and addictions; I thank You, Lord, that they are broken off of us in Jesus' name. We have authority over this stuff and we speak to our temple. We say, 'Body, you line up with the Word of God.' I have authority over you, flesh, and I command you to stop it right now. I command these addictions to be broken off of me right now in Jesus' name. Strongholds be gone. We believe, Father, that for whom the Son has set free is free indeed. We thank You, Lord, for You are so good to us. We consider it done. You're a good God and You're faithful. I hope that was encouraging to y'all today. If you like it, put a thumbs up, click that notification bell, and subscribe. It helps with the algorithms, y'all. When you do that, you're getting the Gospel and videos like this out throughout the world. It helps people start clicking it, and I start getting people who are not born again looking at this; they need Jesus, and you are helping them find Him. I believe with all my heart when they watch my videos that they'll see Jesus loves them and that's going to set them free. Put a smile on your face and a song in your heart. Jesus loves you so much, and we love you. We will see you next time. Y'all have a blessed day. Bye-bye.]
